50.reason: [Error: This is an assignment (=) instead of a comparison (==). It will always be true, causing the block to execute for any input, not just n==5.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: assignment (=) used instead of comparison (==). This sets n to 5 (which is non‑zero, so the condition is always true) and then prints the large block even for smaller n, causing extra output. Should be if(n==5).
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Assignment instead of comparison; sets n to 5, causing condition to be true even for n=1, and then prints all permutations for n=5.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition uses assignment operator '=' instead of equality operator '=='. reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition uses assignment operator '=' instead of equality operator '=='. reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Using assignment operator '=' instead of comparison operator '==', causing n to be set to 5 and the condition always evaluates to true for any input >=1, leading to extra output]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This is an assignment (n=5) instead of a comparison (n==5). This causes the condition to always evaluate to true (since 5 is non-zero), and also modifies the value of n to 5, leading to incorrect output for any input other than 5.]
